assumptions: The dividend receivable gives rise to foreign exchange exposure as a foreign-currency monetary item in the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign exchange recognition under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 B5.7.2 states that foreign exchange gains and losses on foreign-currency monetary assets and liabilities are recognised in profit or loss under IAS 21 unless a hedge-accounting exception applies.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 B5.7.2 identifies a monetary item designated in a cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge-accounting route that changes the default foreign exchange recognition outcome.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 B5.7.2 and IFRIC 16 explicitly address hedges of foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation, with effective hedge results recognised in other comprehensive income and reclassified on disposal.
